title: Define boot handoff and platform description contract
summary: Specify the trusted data passed from loader/firmware into the kernel without binding to one firmware or device tree representation.

title: Implement exceptions, interrupts, timers, and CPU-local state
summary: Handle synchronous faults, interrupts, timers, inter-processor hooks, and per-CPU state through bounded architecture backends.

title: Implement rights attenuation, delegation, and revocation model
summary: Make delegated authority monotonically narrower and revoke future use according to an explicit scalable model.

title: Catalog native platform contracts
summary: Define the minimal typed service families needed by kernel, system services, product runtime, hardware backends, diagnostics, update, and testing.

title: Specify Agent OS IDL syntax and semantic model
summary: Define types, methods, events, errors, handles, ownership, optionality, evolution, resource limits, and documentation needed for native service interfaces.

title: Implement IDL compiler and Rust bindings v0
summary: Generate deterministic client/server bindings, codecs, validation, capability transfer and test fixtures from Agent OS IDL.

title: Define API-level and schema evolution tooling
summary: Track interface levels, compatibility ranges, deprecation, migration, unknown fields, feature discovery, and target support.

title: Specify package, component, and integration manifest
summary: Describe executable components, dependencies, capabilities, data domains, resources, updates, migrations, SBOM, signatures and target compatibility.

title: Specify board package and hardware resource contract
summary: Normalize firmware/board facts into versioned CPU, memory, MMIO, IRQ, clock, reset, power, pin, bus, DMA and device descriptors.

title: Specify device lifecycle, reset, suspend, and hotplug state machine
summary: Give all hardware services consistent discovery, initialization, readiness, degradation, reset, suspend, resume, removal, and failure semantics.

title: Implement block-storage service and backend contract
summary: Expose asynchronous block devices, queues, flush/discard, integrity/error, hot removal, encryption hooks and diagnostics through native services.

title: Implement display, buffer, and compositor backend contract
summary: Provide displays, modes, surfaces, buffer collections, synchronization, presentation timing, capture policy and fallback through native services.

title: Specify portable camera service contract
summary: Represent sensors, streams, controls, metadata, frames, calibration, 3A, processing stages, privacy, timing and power independent of one ISP.

title: Specify portable cellular and telephony service contracts
summary: Separate modem transport, registration, packet data, SMS, SIM/eSIM, GNSS, voice, IMS, emergency and policy into native capability services.

title: Implement power, thermal, battery, charging, and suspend service contracts
summary: Coordinate device power states, wake sources, thermal zones, performance hints, battery/charger safety, energy budgets and suspend transactions.

title: Specify portable device-service quality profiles
summary: Specify portable device-service quality profiles

title: Define entity and relationship schema v0
summary: Specify stable identity, types, relationships, fields, provenance, trust, lifecycle, merge, deletion, and extension rules for first-party entities.

title: Implement provenance and entity-resolution model
summary: Track source assertions, confidence, conflicts, aliases, merges, splits, user overrides, and audit history without erasing origin.

title: Define portable action schema and effect taxonomy
summary: Specify typed inputs/outputs, effect classes, confirmation, idempotency, capabilities, estimates, reversibility, compensation, audit and provider behavior.

title: Assign consistency model to each data family
summary: Choose transaction, event log, CRDT, authoritative provider, snapshot, or ephemeral semantics based on failure and collaboration requirements.

title: Specify action-provider manifests and adapter contracts
summary: Specify action-provider manifests and adapter contracts

title: Freeze portable camera service API level 0
summary: Approve stream/control/result, frame ownership, timestamps, calibration, 3A, processing, privacy, power, lifecycle and extension semantics.

title: Define sensor, lens, calibration, and metadata model
summary: Represent sensor modes, exposure/gain/timing, CFA, black/white levels, lens/AF/OIS, temperature, defects, color/shading and per-module calibration provenance.

title: Specify portable 3A, calibration, and capture-result contracts
summary: Specify portable 3A, calibration, and capture-result contracts

title: Define temporary Pixel sidecar/proxy contracts and retirement plan
summary: Allow selected stock-OS device capabilities to feed experiments through explicit portable proxy services while preserving native replacement boundaries.

title: Publish system threat model v0
summary: Define assets, actors, trust boundaries, physical/network/supply-chain/device/agent threats, assumptions, out-of-scope cases and security objectives.

title: Inventory trusted computing base and privilege domains
summary: Enumerate kernel, loader, recovery, key, update, identity, driver, firmware, secure hardware and privileged service components and minimize their authority.

title: Create formal model and assurance roadmap for kernel core
summary: Model selected capability, IPC, lifecycle, scheduling/resource and memory invariants and choose feasible proof/refinement targets.

title: Establish secure software and model supply-chain process
summary: Apply secure development, review, build provenance, dependency/model/data intake, secrets, release and vulnerability controls across repositories.

title: Create vendor, partner, NDA, and documentation agreement playbook
summary: Standardize technical asks and contract terms for silicon, board, camera, modem, lab, security, and manufacturing partners.

title: Implement OSS/SBOM release compliance pipeline
summary: Automate per-file licensing, dependency review, SBOM, notices, source offers, provenance, and release approval.

title: Commission capability/microkernel architecture review
summary: Obtain expert feedback on object, capability, IPC, revocation, scheduling, resource and assurance semantics before public API stabilization.
